items.summary: CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.

items.title: What is the Index of Services Production and which sectors does it cover?
items.summary: India's Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation has launched a new monthly services production index. This experimental index, with 2024-25 as its base year, covers 19 formal services sub-sectors. The first release for April 2026 shows broad-based growth across most services categories. This new indicator aims to complement the industrial production index for better economic monitoring. The trial series will help assess data quality and methodology before finalization.

items.title: Truecaller CEO: Extending TRAI's jurisdiction encroaches on MeitY's domain
items.summary: Truecaller's CEO Rishit Jhunjhunwala views TRAI's expanded jurisdiction as an encroachment on the IT Ministry's regulatory authority. He argues that compelling IT intermediaries to dictate user information infringes upon existing frameworks. This regulatory dispute centers on the labelling of calls from specific number series. Truecaller maintains that spammers exploit these numbers, violating TRAI's directives. The company advocates for collaborative solutions to enhance consumer protection and transparency.

items.title: PPE failures left NHS staff poorly protected and wasted £10bn, Covid inquiry finds
items.summary: Healthcare staff were unable to properly protect themselves, or those in their care, from dangerous infections, says the latest inquiry report.

items.title: How much should you give to the year-end teacher collection?
items.summary: Collecting money for a joint present might overcome the competitive gift-giving, but it can still cause friction.

items.title: 10 Essential Skills Students Should Learn Before Their First Job
items.summary: A degree may help students qualify for their first job, but succeeding in the real world requires skills that are rarely taught in classrooms. From using AI effectively and communicating ideas with confidence to understanding money and thinking like an entrepreneur, here are 10 essential skills every student should start building before entering the workforce.

items.title: Net direct tax collections rise 16% so far in FY27
items.summary: The higher collections strengthen the Centre's revenue position in the early months of FY27, giving it greater fiscal room to pursue its budgeted spending plans.

items.title: India, Maldives conclude first round of FTA talks; make progress across key negotiating areas
items.summary: India and Maldives concluded their first Free Trade Agreement negotiations. Both nations made substantial progress across eight technical sessions held virtually. Ministers reviewed the talks and reaffirmed commitment to expedite the agreement. Bilateral trade between India and Maldives has seen significant growth. The proposed FTA seeks to enhance market access and economic cooperation.

items.title: Volkswagen Layoff: Auto giant may cut 100,000 jobs despite CEO's 'smarter solutions' promise
items.summary: Volkswagen is considering an additional 50,000 job cuts on top of the existing 50,000 already planned, as part of a major restructuring amid rising pressures from US tariffs, competition in China, and profitability concerns in electric vehicles.

items.title: Govt releases maiden services index; 14 sub-sectors record double-digit growth in April
items.summary: India's new Index of Services Production reveals strong growth in April 2026. Fourteen of nineteen formal services sub-sectors achieved double-digit expansion. Wholesale trade, retail, and accommodation led this impressive economic performance. Air and railway transport experienced declines, while other sectors showed positive annual growth. This index provides crucial monthly data on India's formal services sector.

The silent labour strike: How a worker shortage is threatening India’s industrial leap

items.summary: The Singapore High Court denied Byju’s founder Byju Raveendran's request to halt a six-month jail sentence. Details here.

items.title: Moody's affirms SBI, HDFC Bank ratings on strong asset quality
items.summary: Moody's Ratings has confirmed the ratings for both State Bank of India and HDFC Bank, attributing a stable outlook to their robust asset quality. SBI excels with its diverse lending practices and stable net interest margins, while HDFC Bank is bolstered by a strong retail presence that ensures a low-cost deposit base. The agency forecasts that asset quality for both banks will remain stable moving forward.

items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Stock Price Highlights: Stock market benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty tumbled on Tuesday as a sharp surge in crude oil prices due to the renewed flare-up in West Asia dented investors' sentiment.

items.summary: For many of those retail tech traders, SK Hynix's memory business reminds them of Micron – the paragon of the supply-chain bottleneck thesis.

items.summary: Ashutosh Tiwari, MD and CIO- Public Equities, Equirus Asset Management, highlights how large-cap stocks in India present a favourable risk-reward opportunity amid evolving geopolitical dynamics and changing commodity prices.

items.title: India poised to become one of world's top premium alcobev markets: Diageo India CEO
items.summary: New Delhi is set to become a major premium alcoholic beverage market in the next decade. United Spirits Ltd, also known as Diageo India, plans to lead this significant market evolution. The company sees India's economic expansion creating enduring opportunities for agile and ambitious businesses. Consumers are increasingly trading up to premium brands and engaging with them in new ways.

items.title: PMS Tracker: Equirus, Money Grow Asset, Amaltas, 7 others deliver up to 14% returns in June
items.summary: Portfolio management services delivered mixed June returns, with top strategies gaining up to 14.54% as small-cap and multi-cap funds outperformed. Strong broader markets supported winners, while several multi-cap strategies and select funds declined nearly 10%, reflecting varied performance across investment styles.

items.title: E20 causes no engine damage, no major mileage loss: IIT Kanpur
items.summary: IIT Kanpur researchers report no significant fuel efficiency drop with E20 petrol. Their studies found no evidence of engine damage from the blended fuel. The Oil Ministry also stated E20 offers benefits outweighing minor mileage impacts. Extensive testing has been conducted on ethanol-blended fuels by the institute. Motorists should consult manufacturer manuals and official guidelines for accurate information.

items.title: India refiners reap fuel export windfall as war drives shortages
items.summary: India's oil-product shipments are projected to reach their highest level since September. Refiners are capitalizing on strong profit margins driven by global supply disruptions. Robust profit margins are a result of a Russian export ban and Middle East hostilities. Indian refiners are set to export approximately 1.4 million barrels daily in July. This volume represents a significant increase compared to the previous year and May.

items.title: Is Wall Street heading towards a massive crash by 2026-end? Here's what history may be hinting at
items.summary: Strong US stock gains in 2026 are being overshadowed by historical midterm election volatility, AI valuation concerns and bearish warnings. While corrections are common in midterm years, analysts expect markets to recover strongly after elections, supported by favourable historical trends.

items.title: Insurance distribution boosts fee income at most public sector banks in FY26
items.summary: Public sector banks earned higher insurance commissions in FY26, with most lenders reporting growth. Mutual fund distribution income showed mixed performance across various banks. State Bank of India led insurance commissions, with significant contributions from its subsidiary. Canara Bank and Bank of Baroda also reported insurance commission increases. However, Punjab National Bank and Union Bank of India experienced declines in some segments.

items.title: Global shipping industry sounds the alarm over Trump’s Hormuz toll plan
items.summary: For shipping giant Hapag-Lloyd, it is "fundamentally wrong" to charge tolls for passage through international waters, regardless of the country in charge.

items.title: Two-thirds of India's creators now come from non-metros: Report
items.summary: India's creator economy sees significant growth outside major cities. Most creators still only secure one paid brand campaign annually. This highlights a substantial gap between participation and sustainable income generation. Audience engagement rates have climbed significantly over recent years. Businesses must now focus on long-term creator partnerships for sustainable growth.

items.title: Rupee slides past 96/USD to one-month low as oil climbs; RBI likely intervenes
items.summary: On Tuesday, the Indian rupee fell to its lowest level in over a month, influenced by soaring oil prices and ongoing tensions in the Middle East. The Reserve Bank of India likely stepped in to stabilize the currency as Indian equities also dipped and bond yields rose. Market observers are keeping a close watch on oil fluctuations and central bank measures for any signs of currency recovery.

items.title: SBI Funds Management IPO: How SBI shareholders can improve chances of allotment
items.summary: The SBI Funds Management IPO was subscribed 52% on day one. Eligible SBI shareholders can apply under a reserved quota, competing only with fellow shareholders, potentially improving allotment chances. Investors may also apply separately in the retail category, while post-record-date SBI buyers remain ineligible.

items.title: TCS secures tech contract for New York's JFK Airport's ₹1.8 lakh crore terminal project
items.summary: TCS will develop and manage digital infrastructure for New Terminal One at JFK Airport, part of a $19-billion redevelopment project. Details here.

items.title: 12 stocks held by 100+ MFs in June surged up to 105% in just over 6 months
items.summary: Mutual funds' favourite stocks have rewarded investors handsomely in CY26. ETMarkets' analysis shows that 12 stocks held by over 100 mutual fund schemes each have surged between 50% and 105% so far this year, reflecting strong institutional conviction and robust market momentum.

items.title: California leads lawsuit to block Paramount Warner Bros mega merger
items.summary: The state, where both studios have headquarters, has joined 11 others to halt the deal.

items.title: South East Water must pay £30.5m for supply failures
items.summary: Regulator Ofwat confirms this penalty follows three investigations into the water company.

items.title: HPCL ramps up fuel quality surveillance, finds no adulteration
items.summary: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Ltd intensified fuel quality surveillance across its retail network. Over 3,600 inspections were conducted in the past two weeks to ensure compliance. Surprise checks and regular field inspections verified ethanol blending at outlets. Mobile laboratories also tested fuel samples as part of the extensive monitoring. No instances of adulteration or quality lapses were found during the surveillance period.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 14 July: HCL Tech, Ceat, Swiggy, Anant Raj, Newgen Software Tech among top losers
items.summary: Investor sentiment in India dipped on July 14, driven by rising crude oil prices amid US-Iran tensions, leading to market losses over 0.7%. Nifty Realty and PSU Bank were among the biggest losers, while Nifty Pharma performed positively despite overall market weakness.

items.title: India to ban imports of goods made using forced labour; DGFT notifies new trade rules
items.summary: India has amended its Foreign Trade Policy to ban goods produced using forced labour. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade will conduct inquiries into such production practices. New rules prohibit imports of goods manufactured wholly or partly through forced labour. These provisions align India's trade policy with International Labour Organization definitions.

items.title: Higher education bill may not improve autonomy of HEIs, treats courses inconsistently: Think tank
items.summary: A legislative think tank analysis suggests the Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhishthan Bill, 2025, may not significantly improve higher education autonomy. The bill proposes a unified regulatory commission, dissolving existing bodies like UGC and AICTE. It inconsistently applies to various professional courses, exempting legal and medical programs. Grounds for removing part-time council members are not specified, raising concerns about delegation.

items.title: A French underwear brand is taking on fast fashion — with an IPO
items.summary: Le Slip Français will make its stock market debut in Paris on Tuesday, betting that consumers will pay for locally made clothing amid competition from Shein.

items.summary: Treasury yields increased Tuesday as expectations for more Federal Reserve interest rate hikes grow.

items.title: Vedanta charts $5 billion oil & gas push, targets sharp capacity expansion across businesses
items.summary: Vedanta plans to significantly increase zinc and lead production capacity by 2031. Aluminium production capacity will double over the next three years. The company targets iron and steel output expansion to fifteen million tonnes annually. Vedanta will invest five billion dollars in its oil and gas business. This expansion aims to meet rising domestic demand and strengthen global presence.

items.title: Cube Highways Trust raises ₹1,250 cr from investors ahead of IPO
items.summary: The public issue will close on July 24

items.title: Naveen Jindal Group explores locations in 9 states to set up 18 GW nuclear projects
items.summary: Naveen Jindal Group plans eighteen gigawatts of nuclear power plants. The conglomerate is exploring multiple locations in at least nine states. Discussions are ongoing for partnerships in nuclear power technologies. Investments of lakhs of crores are anticipated for this ambitious venture. This initiative aims to contribute to India's nuclear capacity target.

items.title: Flipkart’s West Bengal story gets a major boost: Company invests in infrastructure, jobs, and market access
items.summary: Flipkart is increasing its investment in West Bengal's digital commerce infrastructure. The company's largest fulfillment center in Haringhata supports this expansion. This initiative empowers local sellers and farmers to reach national markets. Flipkart also invests in skill development and climate-resilient agriculture programs. These efforts strengthen West Bengal's role as a strategic growth partner.

items.summary: HCLTech is betting on AI infrastructure, compute and applications to capture a bigger share of the AI value chain, but investors remain focused on slowing growth and AI-driven pricing pressure.

items.title: India's net direct tax collections rise 16.4% to Rs 6.51 lakh crore as of July 13
items.summary: Net direct tax collections have increased sixteen point four percent year-on-year. Gross direct tax collections also saw a sixteen point one percent rise. Corporate tax collections, after refunds, grew significantly from last year. Non-corporate tax collections and securities transaction tax also climbed. These figures reflect strong tax performance up to July thirteenth.

items.title: Gold rises from two-week low ahead of US inflation data
items.summary: Spot gold was up 0.6% at $4,023.77 per ounce by 0747 GMT, after touching its lowest ‌level since July 1 at $3,983.29

items.title: Aditya Birla Renewables taps MUFG for $1.5 billion to fund Shell asset buy
items.summary: Aditya Birla Renewables is securing significant acquisition financing. Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group is providing $1.5 billion for this deal. This funding will support the purchase of Shell's renewable energy assets. The loan facility has a five-year tenor and is priced competitively. This transaction highlights growing Japanese lender involvement in Indian acquisitions.

items.title: Citi, others dial back India rate hike calls as inflation expected to stay moderate
items.summary: Controlled inflation prompts economists to delay interest rate hike expectations. Retail inflation rose to 4.38% in June, exceeding the RBI's target. Core inflation remained around 4%, suggesting a lower annual average. This shift means the Reserve Bank of India may not tighten policy soon. Future rate hikes depend on sustained core inflation above 4.5%.

items.title: Railways rolls out reform push to speed up projects, boost freight
items.summary: Indian Railways unveiled reforms to enhance project execution and freight operations. Stricter contractor rules and insurance measures aim to improve construction quality. Industries can now develop new freight wagon designs for operational needs. Containerized transport of fly ash and other goods will reduce pollution. Simplified rules and a new land acquisition portal streamline railway operations.

items.title: India's June vegetable oil imports fall 29% on shrinking palm oil discount: SEA
items.summary: India's vegetable oil imports dropped significantly in June, reaching the lowest point this oil year. This decline was primarily driven by reduced palm oil purchases, which saw a notable decrease. Soybean oil imports also fell, while sunflower oil shipments experienced an increase during the same period. Despite the June dip, cumulative imports for the current oil year remain higher than the previous year.

items.title: Why stock market fell today? Rising oil prices among key factors behind Rs 3 lakh crore wealth erosion
items.summary: Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty fell sharply on Tuesday as soaring crude oil prices, renewed rate hike fears, weak Q1 earnings from HCL Tech, a weaker rupee and fragile global cues triggered broad-based selling.

items.title: Capex in mining, construction equipment sectors to hit Rs 10 lakh crore by 2030: Report
items.summary: Capital expenditure for mining and construction equipment will nearly double by 2030. This growth is driven by accelerated infrastructure projects and critical mineral extraction. Domestic original equipment manufacturers must adapt to global technological shifts. India's exports of this equipment already exceed imports, showing strong potential. The nation can expand its global market share by investing in distribution and after-sales services.

items.title: Avendus fund buys PPFAS stake worth Rs 140 crore from promoters
items.summary: Avendus Future Leaders Fund III has acquired nearly a 1% stake in Parag Parikh Financial Advisory Services for around Rs 140 crore through a secondary transaction, backing one of India's largest mutual fund houses.

items.title: Retail inflation may harden in FY27, RBI could hike rates: Crisil
items.summary: Retail inflation in India is projected to increase through the fiscal year. Elevated fuel costs and input prices are driving this upward trend. The Reserve Bank of India will monitor inflation closely for policy adjustments. Food prices face pressure from weather-related risks and higher costs. A potential rate hike is possible if inflationary pressures persist.

items.title: Defence stocks to buy or sell: Kotak expects defence upcycle to continue, prefers HAL over peers
items.summary: India's defence sector is predicted to experience an 11% CAGR in capital expenditure through FY2030, driven by increases in domestic procurement and AoN approvals. Kotak Institutional Equities forecasts a strong push in defence exports, while maintaining cautious views on specific companies.

items.title: Adani family tops India's real estate rich list; DLF remains most valuable listed developer
items.summary: Gautam Adani and family now lead India's real estate tycoons, surpassing Rajiv Singh and family. Their wealth grew significantly while others saw declines amid market corrections. Adani Properties and Prism (OYO) were the largest value creators this year. DLF remains India's most valuable real estate company despite valuation decreases. Mumbai continues to be the nation's real estate capital with substantial valuations.

items.summary: Deutsche Bank, DZ Bank and Groupe BPCE among 36 institutions selected for test of digital currency next year

items.title: Trump's Strait of Hormuz Plan Explained: Can the US charge ships and what it means for India
items.summary: Strait of Hormuz Explained: US President Donald Trump's proposal to impose a 20% charge on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz has sparked a sharp rebuttal from Iran and raised fresh concerns over oil prices, global trade and India's economy. Here's what international law says, why the UN opposes transit tolls, and how the plan could affect India's inflation, crude oil imports, LNG supplies, rupee and fiscal outlook.

items.summary: Education loan assets for NBFCs are projected to grow steadily at twenty percent this fiscal year. Diversification across study destinations offsets demand impact from US policy uncertainties. Asset quality remains robust and is expected to stay stable during this period. US-linked disbursements declined sharply, while UK and other destinations gained traction. The US share of education loan assets fell significantly as of March 31, 2026.

items.title: Higher oil prices to rising bond yields: 5 key risks that could keep the Indian stock market under pressure in 2026
items.summary: As geopolitical tensions and economic indicators cloud the path to recovery, the Indian stock market faces major headwinds. Investors should brace for short-term volatility amidst rising oil prices, bond yields, and inflation pressures that challenge market stability.

items.title: Calcutta Stock Exchange seeks to put exit application on hold
items.summary: The Calcutta Stock Exchange requested Sebi to withhold its exit application. This action follows the West Bengal government's support for reviving the exchange. The exchange plans to formally withdraw its exit application soon. The state government has communicated its revival proposal to the central government. CSE faces significant regulatory and technological challenges ahead.

items.title: Honasa Consumer elevates internal strategist to lead Reginald Men’s parent company

items.summary: The country's largest asset manager is entering the market at a relatively moderate valuation, and its subscription and listing performance could influence how dozens of companies price and time their public issues in the coming months.

items.title: IRCTC appoints Rajneesh Narain as chief financial officer
items.summary: IRCTC appointed Rajneesh Narain as its Chief Financial Officer with immediate effect. Narain's appointment as CFO took effect on July 14, 2026, following board approval. He joined IRCTC as Director (Finance) on June 15, 2026, bringing extensive coal sector experience. Narain previously held finance leadership roles at Northern Coalfields and South Eastern Coalfields. He also served as CFO for subsidiaries of South Eastern Coalfields for six years.

items.title: Wholesale inflation nears double digits in June as food and fuel costs rise

Gujarat's portfolio of GI-tagged agricultural products has now expanded further.

items.title: FCI calls media reports of Rs 1,160 crore ethanol rice diversion 'factually incorrect'

Government agencies detected irregularities and initiated action before media reports surfaced. An FIR was registered, and a joint inspection confirmed a prima facie link. Appropriate legal action will be taken against those found responsible.

items.title: Inflation breaches RBI target as patchy monsoon adds fresh risks

items.title: India's June exports shine through West Asia dark clouds; trade deficit widens to five-month high
items.summary: India's goods exports increased by 15.5% in June, reaching $40.41 billion. However, the trade deficit expanded significantly to $30.43 billion due to higher imports. Oil, gold, and electronics imports drove this substantial increase in the trade deficit. Exports to West Asia showed moderate growth after earlier disruptions from regional conflicts. Free trade pacts and market diversification are boosting India's overall export competitiveness.

items.title: Retail Inflation up to 4.4% on costlier food, transport
items.summary: India's retail inflation reached 4.4% in June, surpassing the central bank's target. Higher food and transport costs drove this increase, marking the highest print under the new CPI series. Food inflation accelerated to 5.3%, crossing the five percent mark for the first time. Core inflation moderated to 3.9%, partly due to falling gold and silver prices. Economists anticipate the Reserve Bank of India will maintain its current policy rate.

items.title: India-UK CETA duty benefits to require CBIC digital authentication
items.summary: India will implement digital authentication for UK exporter origin declarations starting July 15. This new process requires customs to issue a Unique Reference Number before duty benefits are granted. UK exporters must email declarations to designated Indian customs email addresses for verification. Indian customs will authenticate data shared by UK authorities for this purpose. This system aims to prevent fraudulent claims and ensure compliance with trade agreement rules.

items.title: India-EU trade deal legal review nearing completion: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India and the European Union are nearing the end of their trade pact's legal review. This agreement is expected to become operational a few months after the India-UK deal. India's commerce minister noted strong alignment among EU member states on this partnership. He also highlighted significant potential to increase India-Spain bilateral trade and tourism. Both nations aim to boost investments and collaborations in education and talent development.

items.title: US framework trade deal ready, to be inked at the right time: Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal
items.summary: India and the US are progressing well on a proposed trade agreement. The framework deal is ready and will be signed at the right time. Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to a balanced and commercially meaningful agreement. Negotiations are continuing constructively, addressing all aspects of the trade relationship. The trade deal will include pathways for resolving ongoing investigations and preventing new tariffs.

India and Russia anticipate achieving $70 billion in bilateral trade this financial year.

The Directorate General of Foreign Trade issued the public notice regarding these tariff rate quotas.

Cumulative exports for the first quarter also showed an eighteen percent increase. Free trade agreements and market diversification contributed to this strong performance.

items.title: Small biz outstanding grows 13% in FY26, asset quality improves: Report

Sole proprietorship concerns led this significant portfolio expansion. State-run banks showed accelerated enterprise term loan portfolio growth. Manufacturing sector received thirty-one percent of these small business loans.

items.title: India's exports to West Asia region evened out in June: Commerce Secretary
items.summary: India's exports to West Asia grew 7.29 percent to USD 5 billion in June. Shipments to the UAE increased while those to Saudi Arabia saw a decline. Imports from the UAE and Oman registered positive growth during this period. Indian exporters are utilizing Omani ports to facilitate regional trade. The US-Iran conflict had previously impacted international shipping routes.
